title: Jekk'Jekk Tarr

{{Quote|Now there's a place that's literally deadly to our kind.|[[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] bartender|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
The '''Jekk'Jekk Tarr''' was a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] that was located within the [[Nar Shaddaa Docks|spaceport docks]] of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] and circulated [[cyanogen]] fumes along with a variety of other pollutants that were lethal to [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]], but commonplace for, and often preferred by, many other [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Before its establishment as a cantina, it was a ventilation system for passing [[Nagnol/Legends|nagnol gases]] to the docks. It was designed to be a haven for the more exotic species of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]; its very atmosphere providing a buffer against less desirable company. Instead of serving drinks, the bar served "nutrient chemicals" to its clientèle, who paid large sums of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for the privilege.

==Layout==
{{Quote|Humans should stay out of Jekk'Jekk Tarr if they know what's good for them.|A bouncer, to [[Meetra Surik]]|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
The Jekk'Jekk Tarr was divided into five sections, each illuminated with a different light that represented the environment it simulated. Upon entering, a customer would arrive at the infrared chamber, which was a [[Color/Legends|red]] light. This area was favored by [[Species/Legends|species]] that could perceive the infrared, such as the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronians]] and [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]]. Adjacent to the west of this chamber was the yellow, arid room, designed to simulate the harsh climate of worlds such as [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] and [[Sriluur/Legends|Sriluur]]. Because of this, this chamber was frequently inhabited by [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]] and [[Weequay/Legends|Weequays]] alike. To the east of the infrared chamber was a green room of particularly toxic gases. A second toxin room lay to the north of this, and both areas were almost entirely used by the insectoid [[Gand/Legends|Gand]] only. It was likely that the gas in these two rooms was [[Ammonia/Legends|ammonia]] from their home planet [[Gand (planet)/Legends|Gand]]. The fifth chamber was non-toxic and contained no harmful fumes even to [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. It was a private lounge used by the [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Exchange]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Visquis]], and thus it was locked to anybody but Visquis and his servants. In his lounge was the entrance to his secret headquarters, which contained a dimly-illuminated maze with lethal gases and a jamming system to ensure that datapad maps were rendered useless.<ref name="kotor2" />

==History==
{{Quote|Ah, what a prime example of human arrogance. What you know is substantially less than what I know. Otherwise, you would not have come. One, everything that goes on in the Jekk'Jekk Tarr is invisible to Goto. It is something I discovered by accident, and have tested many times since to ensure accuracy. Second, I am not acting on Goto's orders—not anymore. Hanharr and I reached an agreement to deal with Goto altogether... and collect a tidy sum from Vogga.|translated=1|Visquis to the Bounty Hunter Mira|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
The Exchange crime lord Visquis would often use the bar for dealings he wanted kept secret from [[G0-T0|Goto]], having discovered accidentally at some point that Goto could not monitor events that happened inside the Jekk'Jekk Tarr. This was because [[Droid/Legends|droids]] did not function properly inside of it. During her time on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] in [[3951 BBY]], the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] was lured here by Visquis in his attempt to claim the Exchange's outstanding [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. She survived the hostile environment using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], harnessing the [[Force power/Legends|power]] of [[breath control]] in order to conserve oxygen and to prevent the gases from being inhaled. When Visquis saw that the gas had no effect on her, he commanded the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and other bar patrons within to attack her, offering a huge reward to whoever took her down. She was attacked by a [[Unidentified Devaronian bartender|Devaronian bartender]] and a [[Unidentified Twi'lek majordomo (Nar Shaddaa)|Twi'lek majordomo]], among others, before eventually reaching Visquis's private room. During the battle, she convinced the ''[[Lunar Shadow]]'' captain to go back to his crew before continuing her pursuit through the tunnels below the cantina and into Visquis's lair. Upon returning at a later time, the Exile found that the Jekk'Jekk Tarr had been closed.<ref name="kotor2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Jekk'Jekk Tarr first appears in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. The cantina and the base below it were originally intended to have been one of the lairs of the secretive bounty hunter guild known as the [[GenoHaradan]]. However, this was cut from the final game and the base became the stronghold of Visquis' faction of the Exchange.<ref name="tg">[http://forums.team-gizka.org/viewtopic.php?f=5&t=1961 A list of cut content] provided by [[Cut content from Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords|The Sith Lords Restoration Project]] web forum.</ref>

The player can enter the Jekk'Jekk Tarr without a spacesuit before the quest to rescue Mira activates without any consequences to the Exile's health, provided that she is equipped with a gas mask; if this is done, she can contact the ''Lunar Shadow'' captain before the bar patrons become hostile. The Exile can also recruit [[Odis]] to be the new captain for the ''Lunar Shadow'' crew before entering the cantina. Nevertheless, the former captain cannot be killed, and is the only patron to remain neutral during the skirmish.<ref name="guide">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref>

''Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'', the only source thus far in which the Jekk'Jekk Tarr appears, misspells the cantina's name once as ''Jek'Jek Tarr'', within the Music list accessible from the game's menu screen.<ref name="kotor2" />
